{"key_event":"Postwar stabilization after fiscal and social mobilization","reasoning":"Civil society recovered from wartime constraint through parties, unions, veterans’ associations, elections, and social-rights claims, though labor splits, strike defeat, and demographic losses kept it below its prewar peak. The state remained very strong through reconstruction, taxation, colonial coercion, monetary stabilization, and expanded welfare regulation, but it fell back from wartime near-total command because of debt, franc instability, cabinet instability, and manpower losses.","society_change":0.6,"society_power":8.2,"state_change":-0.5,"state_power":8.9}
